content: Hi, I'm Sage, and I'm a Librarian at a university research library. I'm feeling really frustrated with my current role and I'm not sure if this is the right career for me anymore.
Hi Sage, sorry to hear that you're feeling frustrated. Can you tell me more about what's leading to these feelings? Is it the work itself, the university, or something else?

I think it's a combination of everything. I love working with books and helping students, but the administrative tasks and budget cuts are taking a toll on me. I'm also feeling really undervalued and unappreciated in my current role.
It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed and undervalued in your current role. Have you considered exploring other areas of librarianship, or possibly making a career change altogether?

Yeah, I've been thinking about that a lot lately. I've always been interested in surfing, and I've even started taking some lessons on the side. Maybe I could find a way to use my love of the ocean to help others.

That's a great idea! Using your love of the ocean to help others is a wonderful way to make a positive impact. What kind of lessons have you been taking, and what do you hope to achieve with your surfing?

I've been taking lessons at a local surf school, and I'm really enjoying it. I'd love to start competing in some local competitions and see where it takes me. I'm thinking of investing in a new surfboard and practicing regularly.

It sounds like you're really committed to pursuing your passion for surfing. Investing in a new surfboard and practicing regularly are great steps to take. Have you thought about how you'll balance your surfing with your current work as a Librarian?

To be honest, I'm not sure. I'm thinking of scaling back my hours as a Librarian and focusing more on my surfing. I've got a competition coming up on July 22nd, and I'm hoping it will be a good opportunity to get my name out there. I'm also planning a trip to Hawaii in September to surf some of the best waves in the world.

It's great that you have a competition lined up! Scaling back your hours as a Librarian could be a good way to free up time and energy to focus on your surfing. What's the plan for your trip to Hawaii, and how are you preparing for it?

I'm really excited about the trip! I've been training for a 5K and I'm hoping to be in top physical condition for the surfing. I'm planning to spend a few weeks on the North Shore and then head to Maui to explore the island and surf some of the best waves.

That sounds like an amazing adventure! Training for a 5K and surfing some of the best waves in the world will be a great challenge, but it's also a wonderful opportunity to grow and learn. What do you hope to achieve with your surfing in Hawaii, and how do you think it will be received?

I hope to be able to use my surfing to connect with people and showcase the beauty of the ocean. I'm a bit nervous about how it will be received, but I'm excited to take the risk and see what happens.

It's great that you're taking the risk and pursuing your passion. Remember that surfing is a universal language, and people will appreciate your efforts to ride the waves and have fun. What's the most challenging part of preparing for your trip to Hawaii, and how are you overcoming those challenges?
I think the most challenging part is balancing my training for the 5K and my surfing. But I'm excited to learn and adapt, and I'm sure it will be a valuable experience.

It's great that you're aware of the challenges and are preparing yourself for them. Balancing your training and surfing can be difficult, but it's also a great opportunity to grow and learn. What do you think you'll miss most about your life on the mainland when you're in Hawaii?
I think I'll miss my family and friends the most. But I'm excited to make new connections and friendships in Hawaii and to pursue my passion for surfing.

It's great that you're excited about the opportunity to make new connections and friendships. Remember that it's okay to take risks and pursue your passions, and that you can always stay in touch with your loved ones back on the mainland.
